title: hieracium nigrescens subsp. glabriligulatum
intro: Hieracium nigrescens subsp. glabriligulatum, also known as the black hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium nigrescens plant, characterized by its dark green leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of black hawkweed include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of black hawkweed can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions.
